author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
title: What Purpose the Law Subserves.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: What object, then, can this man gain by accounting the law and the teaching to be the grace whereby we are helped to work righteousness?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: For, in order that it may help much, it must help us to feel our need of grace.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: No man, indeed, is able to fulfil the law through the law.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: “Love is the fulfilling of the law.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: And the love of God is not shed abroad in our hearts by the law, but by the Holy Ghost, which is given unto us.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Grace, therefore, is pointed at by the law, in order that the law may be fulfilled by grace.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Now what does it avail for Pelagius, that he declares the self-same thing under different phrases, that he may not be understood to place in law and teaching that grace which, as he avers, assists the “capacity” of our nature?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: So far, indeed, as I can conjecture, the reason why he fears being so understood is, because he condemned all those who maintain that God’s grace and help are not given for a man’s single actions, but exist rather in his freedom, or in the law and teaching.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: And yet he supposes that he escapes detection by the shifts he so constantly employs for disguising what he means by his formula of “law and teaching” under so many various phrases.
